text.title: Agent Architectures (Memory + Tools + Planning + Reflection) Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of agent-architectures enforces. Each rule has a statement, a rationale anchored to industry practice, and a source citation.

rule.statement: Memory is summarized when raw recent-context exceeds 30% of the model's context window.
rule.rationale: Raw concatenation overflows at 50+ iterations; 30% leaves room for planning + tool I/O.

rule.statement: Tool registry validates inputs against a JSON Schema before dispatch; reject + return typed error on mismatch.
rule.rationale: Schema-validated dispatch eliminates the largest class of agent failures (malformed tool calls).

rule.statement: State machine has explicit `planning`, `acting`, `reflecting`, `error`, `done` states; transitions are logged.
rule.rationale: Implicit state in a while-loop produces unobservable agents; explicit FSM makes debugging tractable.

rule.statement: Reflection step compares last-N tool results to the original goal; if drift detected, re-plan instead of continuing.
rule.rationale: Without reflection, agents loop on stale plans for dozens of iterations.

rule.statement: Idle-eviction policy: if no progress on goal for K iterations, kill the agent and escalate to human.
rule.rationale: Without escalation, agents burn unbounded cost on stuck states.
